_meta.notes: Schema v3: ingredients have unit/qty for servings scaling. Steps have timer_seconds for cook mode.

beggar_original.label: Beggar Original
beggar_original.description: Mashups and inventions you won't find anywhere else

restaurant_to_home.label: Restaurant → Home
restaurant_to_home.description: Cap that overpriced restaurant dish — make it at home

recipes.name: Dubai Chocolate Mochi
recipes.tagline: Two viral hits in one — Dubai's $18 chocolate bar meets the rice paper mochi trend. $5 to make, healthier than original.

restaurant_price.context: The viral $18 Dubai chocolate bar by FiX. Lady M's pistachio version is similar.

recipes.why_this_works: Combines two viral foodtok trends: Dubai's $18 pistachio chocolate bar and the rice paper mochi hack. Uses pre-toasted kataifi (skip 7 min of toasting) and a drizzle instead of full chocolate coating — much easier than the original recipe. Greek yogurt base means higher protein, lower sugar, 70% cheaper.

ingredients.name: Rice paper sheets
ingredients.amazon_search: vietnamese rice paper spring roll wrappers

ingredients.name: Toasted kataifi (pre-roasted)
ingredients.note: Buy pre-toasted to save 7 minutes. Brands like Eastanbul, Moosh, Ceres.

ingredients.name: Greek yogurt (full fat)
ingredients.amazon_search: fage greek yogurt full fat 5 percent
ingredients.note: FULL FAT only — low-fat ruins texture

ingredients.name: Dark chocolate (70%+)
ingredients.note: Half goes in filling, half for drizzle

instructions.title: Make the filling
instructions.content: Melt 50g chocolate (microwave 30 sec, stir, repeat if needed). Mix in a bowl: 1 cup toasted kataifi + 1/2 cup pistachio cream + 1 tbsp melted butter + the melted chocolate. Stir until evenly coated. Save the remaining 50g chocolate for drizzling later.

instructions.title: Prep your station
instructions.content: Set up: a bowl of cold water, a wet plate or damp towel, the filling, greek yogurt, and a tray lined with parchment paper. You'll work fast — rice paper softens in seconds.

instructions.title: Wrap each mochi
instructions.content: Dip one rice paper sheet in cold water for exactly 3 seconds. Lay flat. Don't worry if it still feels firm — it keeps softening. Add 2 tbsp greek yogurt in center, then top with 1 tbsp filling. Fold all four sides over the filling, then flip seam-side down. Should look like a small dumpling pouch. Place on parchment. Repeat for all 8 sheets.

instructions.title: Drizzle chocolate (optional)
instructions.content: Melt the remaining 50g chocolate. Use a spoon or fork to drizzle thin stripes across each mochi. Don't fully cover — drizzling is faster than dipping and looks more pro. Skip this step entirely if you want the simplest version.

instructions.title: Freeze and serve
instructions.content: Freeze on parchment for 1 hour minimum. Serve cold straight from freezer — texture is best within 5 minutes of removing.

pro_tips: Buy pre-toasted kataifi. Toasting from raw takes 7 minutes and is easy to burn. Pre-toasted is sold on Amazon — search 'toasted kataifi'.
Full-fat greek yogurt is non-negotiable. Low-fat versions release water and ruin the chewy texture.
Don't oversoak rice paper. 3 seconds in cold water is plenty — it keeps softening on the plate.
Drizzling chocolate is much easier than coating. Use a fork dipped in melted chocolate, then flick across the mochi.
Eat within 24 hours of freezing for best texture. Rice paper gets too hard if frozen longer.

variations.name: Strawberry Dubai Mochi
variations.change: Add 1 diced strawberry to filling per mochi. Adds tartness.

variations.name: Nutella Mochi
variations.change: Swap pistachio cream for Nutella. Different flavor, same technique.

variations.name: Salted Caramel
variations.change: Sprinkle flaky sea salt on chocolate while wet. Salted Dubai vibe.

variations.name: Lower Calorie
variations.change: Use 2 oz chocolate instead of 100g. Drop to ~190 cal/serving.

videos.url_search: dubai chocolate mochi rice paper recipe
videos.title: Tutorials on YouTube

recipes.background: The Dubai chocolate bar by FiX Dessert Chocolatier went viral on TikTok in late 2023, with one creator's 74M-view video pushing the $18 bar into global fame. The bar features pistachio cream and crispy kataifi (shredded phyllo) inside chocolate. Meanwhile, the rice paper mochi hack — wrapping frozen yogurt/fillings in softened rice paper — became its own trend in 2024-2025. This recipe combines both for a homemade version that costs $5 and adds protein from greek yogurt.

recipes.name: Chipotle Burrito Bowl
recipes.tagline: The secret is the sauce. Make 4 bowls for the price of 1 at Chipotle. Smoky chipotle crema does the heavy lifting.

restaurant_price.context: Chicken bowl with guacamole. Lunch standard in every business district.

recipes.why_this_works: The real Chipotle secret is the smoky chipotle crema (greek yogurt + adobo sauce + lime). Make it once, use it all week — on bowls, tacos, eggs, anything. Everything else is just store-bought shortcuts: Ben's Ready Rice (90 sec), canned beans (1 min), pre-made guac. The sauce is what makes it taste like Chipotle.

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs (boneless skinless)
ingredients.note: Thighs > breast for this. Chipotle uses thighs.

ingredients.name: Chipotle marinade
ingredients.amazon_search: mccormick grill mates chipotle pepper marinade
ingredients.note: McCormick Grill Mates Chipotle Pepper — saves the spice measuring

ingredients.name: Greek yogurt (full fat)
ingredients.amazon_search: fage greek yogurt full fat 5 percent
ingredients.note: This becomes the secret sauce base

ingredients.name: Chipotle peppers in adobo sauce
ingredients.amazon_search: la costena chipotle peppers in adobo sauce
ingredients.note: THE MVP. One can = 8 batches of sauce. Lasts months in fridge.

ingredients.name: Lime
ingredients.note: Fresh lime juice — don't use bottled

ingredients.name: Ready Rice Cilantro Lime
ingredients.amazon_search: uncle bens ready rice cilantro lime
ingredients.note: 90 seconds in microwave. No actual cooking needed.

ingredients.name: Black beans (canned)
ingredients.amazon_search: goya black beans low sodium canned
ingredients.note: Goya or any low-sodium brand. Drain and rinse.

ingredients.name: Pre-made guacamole
ingredients.note: Wholly Guacamole. Skip making your own.

ingredients.name: Salsa or pico de gallo
ingredients.note: Any tomato salsa. Or get the corn salsa for that Chipotle vibe.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.note: Goes in the sauce. Most kitchens already have it.

instructions.title: Make THE SAUCE (the secret weapon)
instructions.content: In a blender or bowl: 1 cup greek yogurt + 3 chipotle peppers (from the can, with sauce) + juice of 1 lime + 1 tbsp honey + 1/2 tsp salt. Blend or whisk until smooth. Done. This sauce lasts 5 days in fridge and makes EVERYTHING taste like Chipotle. Tacos, eggs, sandwiches, anything.

instructions.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions.content: Cut chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl: chicken + McCormick chipotle marinade packet + juice of 1 lime + 2 tbsp olive oil. Toss to coat. Let sit 30 minutes (or overnight in fridge for stronger flavor).

instructions.title: Cook the chicken
instructions.content: Heat a pan or skillet medium-high. Add marinated chicken in a single layer. Cook 4 minutes, flip, cook 3-4 more minutes until charred edges and cooked through (165°F internal). Set aside.

instructions.title: Heat the base (90 seconds)
instructions.content: Microwave 2 pouches of Ben's Ready Rice Cilantro Lime per package (usually 90 seconds). Drain and rinse black beans, then microwave for 1 minute in a bowl with a pinch of salt and cumin.

instructions.title: Assemble 4 bowls
instructions.content: Divide into 4 bowls: rice → beans → chicken → spoonful of guacamole → salsa → DRIZZLE THE SAUCE GENEROUSLY. The sauce is the move. Don't be shy with it.

pro_tips: Make a double batch of the sauce. You'll use it on everything for the next 5 days — eggs, tacos, sandwiches, grilled veggies. This is the real win.
💡 Even faster: Skip steps 2-3 — grab a rotisserie chicken on the way home, shred it, toss with the marinade. 5 minutes total.
Buy 1 can of chipotle peppers in adobo. Freeze leftover peppers in an ice cube tray with their sauce. One cube = one sauce batch.
Chicken thighs > breast for this. Stays juicy even after meal prep.
For corn salsa vibes (real Chipotle): mix frozen sweet corn + lime + cilantro + diced red onion.

variations.name: Vegetarian Bowl
variations.change: Swap chicken for sofritas-style tofu or extra beans. Sauce makes anything taste good.

variations.name: Steak Bowl
variations.change: Sub chicken for flank steak strips. Marinate same way. Cook hotter and faster.

variations.name: Low-Carb
variations.change: Skip the rice, double the beans. Add cauliflower rice underneath.

variations.name: Burrito version
variations.change: Wrap everything in a large flour tortilla instead of bowl. Toast the wrap for 30 sec on each side.

videos.url_search: chipotle copycat burrito bowl secret sauce
videos.title: Copycat tutorials on YouTube

recipes.background: Chipotle's lunch bowl runs $11-14 in NYC, and lines wrap around the block at noon. The real secret isn't the rice or beans — it's the chipotle crema some locations add. TikTok creators like @thatsavagekitchen (95K+ likes) cracked the code: greek yogurt + chipotle peppers in adobo + lime + honey. Make it once, use it all week. One can of chipotle peppers ($3) makes 8 batches of sauce. That's how 4 bowls cost the price of one Chipotle bowl.
